title: Meters to Feet Converter
shortDescription: Convert meters to feet with a precise factor and clear results for height, plans, and measurements.
metaDescription: Convert meters to feet using the standard factor 1 m = 3.28084 ft for heights, plans, and project measurements.
intro: Enter a value in meters to get the equivalent in feet using the standard conversion factor 1 meter = 3.28084 feet.
overview:
Use this converter when you need meter values expressed in feet for height, room dimensions, drawings, or technical specs. It applies the standard metric-to-imperial conversion so the result is directly usable in notes, plans, or comparisons.

The tool is helpful when a measurement is written in meters but the receiving context expects feet. You can check a single value, compare several dimensions, or convert decimal values without doing the arithmetic by hand.
howItWorks: Type a number in meters into the input field.
The tool multiplies the value by 3.28084 to calculate feet.
Read the result in feet and use it in your measurement context.

useCases.title: Height written in metric units
useCases.description: Convert a person’s or object’s height from meters into feet for documents, profiles, or forms.

useCases.title: Room and wall dimensions
useCases.description: Translate floor plans or room sizes from meters into feet before comparing layouts.

useCases.title: Construction and renovation notes
useCases.description: Turn meter-based site measurements into feet when a project uses imperial units.

useCases.title: Equipment and shipping specs
useCases.description: Convert product or cargo dimensions from meters to feet for technical sheets and logistics.

examples.title: Apartment ceiling height
examples.description: Useful for reading room height in a format that uses feet.

examples.title: Garden fence length
examples.description: Shows a larger measurement often needed in outdoor planning.

examples.title: Small object size
examples.description: Helpful for decimal measurements that are awkward to estimate manually.

faq.question: What factor does the converter use?
faq.answer: It uses the standard relation 1 meter = 3.28084 feet.

faq.question: How are decimal meters handled?
faq.answer: Decimal values are converted directly, so 1.25 meters becomes 4.1011 feet after rounding.

faq.question: Why might the result differ from a rough estimate?
faq.answer: Feet are often estimated using simple mental math, but this tool uses the full conversion factor, so the displayed value is more exact.

faq.question: Can I convert very small or very large values?
faq.answer: Yes. The same factor works for tiny measurements and large dimensions; only the displayed precision may matter for your use case.

faq.question: What mistake should I avoid when reading the result?
faq.answer: Do not confuse feet with inches. If you need inches, convert the feet result again or use a dedicated inches converter.
